question: Will Nasdaq 100 (NDX) hit $30,000 (HIGH) in December?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for December 2026, any 1-minute candle for Nasdaq 100 (NDX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for Nasdaq 100 (NDX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5ENDX/.

events.title: What will Nasdaq 100 (NDX) hit by end of December?
events.description: What will Nasdaq 100 (NDX) hit by end of December 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a modest year-end rally for the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), with odds favoring levels above 20,500 by December 31, fueled by post-election optimism and expectations of a Federal Reserve rate cut at the December 18 FOMC meeting. Currently hovering near 20,200 after a 25% YTD gain driven by AI-fueled Big Tech strength, NDX faces tailwinds from easing monetary policy—traders price in 90% odds of a 25bps cut amid cooling inflation (October CPI at 2.6% YoY)—but risks persist from potential fiscal stimulus inflating yields or geopolitical tensions. Watch December 11 CPI and December 6 NFP for volatility, as sustained 4-5% 10-year Treasury yields could cap upside.

question: Will Nasdaq 100 (NDX) close at <$23,500 in December?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Nasdaq 100 (NDX) on the final trading day of December 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5ENDX/history, published under "Historical Prices."

events.title: What will Nasdaq 100 (NDX) close at in December?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Nasdaq 100 (NDX) on the final trading day of December 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on the Nasdaq 100's December close remains tightly contested, with the >$36,000 bin edging out at 41.8% implied probability amid a cluster of mid-range outcomes ($23,500-$30,500 bands) hovering at 40-41.5%, reflecting polarized views on tech-driven upside versus macroeconomic headwinds. Primary catalysts include the December 18 FOMC meeting, where markets price a 75% chance of a 25bps rate cut per CME FedWatch, potentially fueling AI-fueled rallies in Magnificent Seven stocks if dot-plot projections signal aggressive 2025 easing. Countering this, persistent inflation signals from recent CPI data (November at 2.7% YoY) and softening consumer spending raise recession risks, capping gains below $30,000; historical end-of-year seasonality favors modest 5-10% upside from current ~21,000 levels, but election afterglow has faded, amplifying volatility ahead of key retail sales and PPI releases.

question: Will the US acquire part of Greenland in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States acquires control of any land territory that is part of Greenland by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only the transfer of sovereignty, or the acquisition of primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control qualifies.

1. Transfer of Sovereignty: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument results in a defined area of Greenland coming under the formal sovereignty of the U.S. (e.g., incorporated as a U.S. state, territory, possession, or other U.S. political classification), even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline.

2. Acquisition of Primary or Exclusive Jurisdiction or Control: This will qualify if a binding agreement or legal instrument establishes a defined area in Greenland in which the U.S. has primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over the territory, such that the ordinary legal authority of Denmark and Greenland do not apply,except by U.S. permission. Such agreements or instruments will qualify even if the effective date occurs after the market deadline. 

3. Use of Force: If the U.S. acquires primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control over a defined area of Greenland through force, this will also qualify. 

An announcement will qualify only if it is accompanied by or consists of a binding agreement or legal instrument  (e.g., enacted legislation, a signed treaty, the signed text of an agreement, or an executive action implementing such an agreement) that unambiguously creates a transfer of sovereignty, or primary or exclusive jurisdiction or control,  even if this transfer or acquisition takes effect after the market deadline. 

Non-binding statements, negotiations, proposals, frameworks, or MOUs will not alone qualify. Basing rights, access agreements, SOFA-type arrangements, COFA-type arrangements, commercial concessions, or other permissions to use land (including leases) will not alone qualify. Any qualifying U.S. jurisdiction or control  in Greenland that existed at market creation will not count as new qualifying control.

Examples of qualifying events include but are not limited to treaty or piece of legislation that makes any portion of Greenland a U.S. territory or possession, even if the handover date for such territory or possession is later); or, a Guantánamo-style arrangement establishing a defined zone in Greenland under exclusive or primary U.S. jurisdiction and control, where Denmark and Greenland’s ordinary legal authority does not apply except by U.S. permission.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States, Denmark, and Greenland; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Denmark and Greenland officials have firmly rejected any US acquisition proposals, reinforcing the territory's status within the Kingdom of Denmark and underscoring international sovereignty norms that make territorial purchase highly improbable. Recent statements from President-elect Trump in late December 2024 reiterated interest in Greenland for national security reasons, but Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen immediately reaffirmed her 2019 stance of "no, no, no," while Greenland Premier Múte B. Egede emphasized self-determination. Absent diplomatic breakthroughs or referendums—none scheduled—traders price an 84.5% "No" probability, viewing rhetorical interest as insufficient against legal and political barriers, consistent with historical precedents like failed past overtures.

question: Will Cristiano Ronaldo announce his retirement in 2026?
description:
This polymarket will resolve to "Yes" if Cristiano Ronaldo officially announces his retirement from club soccer by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To resolve "Yes", the announcement must clearly state that Ronaldo is retiring from club-level professional soccer. The announcement must come from Ronaldo himself or his official representatives.

Retirement from international soccer alone will not qualify. 

The retirement must be intended to take effect immediately or prior to the start of the next club season following the announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Cristiano Ronaldo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will Venezuelan crude oil production reach 1m barrels per day in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes”, if Venezuelan crude oil production is greater than or equal to the listed number of barrels per day for any month in 2026, according to the OPEC Monthly Oil Market Report published for each month.

The resolution source for this market will be the OPEC Monthly Oil Market Report, published each month in reference to the previous month at https://www.opec.org/monthly-oil-market-report.html. The relevant figure can be found in “Table 5-7 DoC crude oil production based on secondary sources, tb/d” under the column for the relevant month and the “Venezuela” row.

This market will resolve as soon as Venezuelan crude oil production is reported to be greater than or equal to the listed number. question: Will the Court Force Trump to Refund Tariffs?
description:
On May 28, 2025, the U.S. Court of International Trade ruled that Donald Trump exceeded his authority under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) by imposing a series of broad tariffs. The ruling blocked several major measures, including the “Liberation Day” tariffs—a 10% tariff on all imports and country-specific rates of up to 50%—as well as additional tariffs targeting Canadian, Mexican, and Chinese goods. The Trump administration has filed a single consolidated appeal of this decision, titled V.O.S. Selections, Inc. v. United States.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if, by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, both of the following occur:

1. The Trump administration’s appeal in V.O.S. Selections, Inc. v. United States is denied, in whole or in part

2. U.S. importers receive refunds of at least some tariffs invalidated by the May 28, 2025 ruling, where such refunds occur as a consequence of the denial, in whole or in part, of the Trump administration’s appeal.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Announcements, court orders, or plans to issue refunds will not be sufficient for resolution unless actual refunds are issued within the market timeframe.

For purposes of this market, a “refund” includes direct payments, credits, or offsets issued to importers by U.S. Customs and Border Protection or the U.S. Treasury reflecting repayment of previously collected tariffs.

If the appeal is fully upheld and no refunds are issued, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official government or court information, or a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: The absence of a final court ruling mandating refunds for Trump-era Section 232 and 301 tariffs drives the 69% "No" implied probability, as traders assess low near-term risk of forced repayments. Recent US Court of International Trade decisions struck down select tariffs on steel, aluminum, and Chinese goods as exceeding presidential authority, but the Federal Circuit's stays and partial affirmances in late 2024 have delayed implementation, preserving government revenue collection. Incoming Trump administration policy signals suggest potential tariff expansions rather than reversals, while prolonged appeals reduce urgency. Consensus reflects litigation timelines outpacing market resolution dates, with no Supreme Court intervention scheduled.

eventMetadata.context_description: MrBeast's explosive growth trajectory, fueled by weekly mega-viral challenges averaging 150 million views per video, anchors trader consensus at strong yes odds for reaching the billion-view milestone by March 31. His main channel sits at approximately 32 billion lifetime views as of late February, with recent hits like "7 Days Stranded" surging 200 million views in under two weeks amid YouTube algorithm favoritism for high-engagement content. Industry dynamics favor him—nonstop Shorts amplification and cross-promotions via Feastables boost momentum—while upcoming releases, including a teased survival series, could accelerate gains. Traders eye daily accrual rates (50-100 million) against the tight deadline, though algorithm shifts or upload delays pose risks in this unpredictable creator economy.

question: EU debt downgrade before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the European Union's long-term sovereign credit letter rating is downgraded by any of the three major credit rating agencies (S&P, Moody's, Fitch) at any point between market creation and December 31, 2026 11:59pm ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: EU debt downgrade before 2027?

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Standard & Poor's, Moody's, or Fitch, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a razor-thin 52.5% implied probability of an EU sovereign debt downgrade before 2027, driven primarily by escalating fiscal strains in high-debt nations like France (112% debt-to-GDP) and Italy (140%), where widening deficits and political instability have pushed 10-year bond yields above 3%. Rating agencies such as Moody's and S&P maintain negative outlooks amid sluggish Eurozone growth and ECB rate cuts offering only partial relief. This balance stems from the ECB's implicit backstop versus stricter EU fiscal rules enforcement in 2025. Key tipping points include France's 2025 budget vote in December, Q4 GDP releases, and January rating reviews, any signaling unchecked borrowing could surge Yes odds above 60%.

question: Will United Russia (ER) win the most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

Recent September 2024 regional parliamentary votes reinforced this, with United Russia capturing over 90% of seats across multiple regions amid low turnout and limited competition. Traders price in minimal upset risk due to President Putin's consolidated power and suppression of dissent. Realistic challenges—such as major Ukraine war reversals or widespread unrest—remain low-probability tail risks absent evidence of momentum.

question: Will Beyoncé perform at the 2026 Todo Mundo no Rio music festival?
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed artists who perform live and in person at the Todo Mundo no Rio 2026 music festival, currently expected to take place in April or May. Appearances during which the listed artist does not perform will not count towards the resolution of this market.

If this event is cancelled or otherwise delayed beyond July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be footage and information from Todo Mundo no Rio 2026 festival, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Brazilian pop icon Anitta to headline Todo Mundo no Rio 2026, with implied probabilities around 55%, driven by her recent Instagram post hinting at a massive Rio return amid her global tour success and local fan fervor. Official lineup remains unannounced, but festival organizers' pattern of blending homegrown talent like Ludmilla with international draws like Bad Bunny adds uncertainty—past editions featured surprise guests post-Carnival buzz. Key watch: expected performer reveal by Q4 2025 presser, potentially shifting odds as streaming metrics and ticket presales signal demand; award secret votes and chart volatility underscore entertainment's unpredictability for traders.

If this event is cancelled or otherwise delayed beyond July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be footage and information from Todo Mundo no Rio 2026 festival, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Brazilian pop icon Anitta to headline Todo Mundo no Rio 2026, with implied probabilities around 55%, driven by her recent Instagram post hinting at a massive Rio return amid her global tour success and local fan fervor. Official lineup remains unannounced, but festival organizers' pattern of blending homegrown talent like Ludmilla with international draws like Bad Bunny adds uncertainty—past editions featured surprise guests post-Carnival buzz. Key watch: expected performer reveal by Q4 2025 presser, potentially shifting odds as streaming metrics and ticket presales signal demand; award secret votes and chart volatility underscore entertainment's unpredictability for traders.

question: Will the WNBA and WNBPA sign a new CBA by Mar. 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the date on which the WNBA and WNBA players association sign a new collective bargaining agreement (CBA). The listed market will resolve to "Yes" if the date of signing is between market creation and the listed date 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, the listed market will resolve to "No."

For purposes of this market, a CBA is considered ‘executed’ only when the final written agreement has been formally signed by authorized representatives of both the WNBA and the WNBA Players Association. Tentative agreements, ratifications, or agreements pending signature do not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: New WNBA CBA agreement by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the date on which the WNBA and WNBA players association sign a new collective bargaining agreement (CBA). The listed market will resolve to "Yes" if the date of signing is between market creation and the listed date 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, the listed market will resolve to "No."

For purposes of this market, a CBA is considered ‘executed’ only when the final written agreement has been formally signed by authorized representatives of both the WNBA and the WNBA Players Association. Tentative agreements, ratifications, or agreements pending signature do not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Negotiations between the WNBA and WNBPA remain the dominant driver of trader consensus for a new CBA before the current deal expires after 2025, with markets pricing a roughly 40% implied probability of agreement by year-end amid steady but tense bargaining sessions. Recent developments include four formal meetings since July, including a key October 10 session where the league offered a 50% revenue share—up from 10-20%—though players push for higher amid booming media rights and expansion like the 2025 Golden State Valkyries. Sticking points involve salary caps and pension benefits; optimism from commissioner Cathy Engelbert tempers lockout risks, but delays could spill into the May preseason if unresolved by Q1 2026.

question: Will United Russia (ER) gain the most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that gains the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election, compared to before the election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats gained, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats gained by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

events.title: Which party will gain most seats in Russian Parliamentary Election?
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that gains the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election, compared to before the election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats gained, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats gained by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors United Russia (72.5%) to secure the most seats in Russia's upcoming State Duma election, driven by its entrenched incumbency, state media dominance, and control over single-mandate districts, consistent with results in 2021 when it won 324 seats. Recent VCIOM and FOM polls (August 2024) show United Russia at 40-45% support, far ahead of rivals amid suppressed opposition following arrests of figures like Alexei Navalny and tightened electoral laws. New People (17.6%) gains traction from youth appeal and regional wins in 2023 gubernatorial races, boosting its party-list prospects under leader Alexei Nechayev. LDPR (7.0%) and KPRF (1.7%) trail due to post-Zhirinovsky leadership flux and static bases, while smaller parties like SRZP, Rodina, and Civic Platform face steep barriers. Upcoming party congresses and candidate approvals could shift dynamics before the 2026 vote.

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats gained, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats gained by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

events.title: Which party will gain most seats in Russian Parliamentary Election?
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors United Russia (72.5%) to secure the most seats in Russia's upcoming State Duma election, driven by its entrenched incumbency, state media dominance, and control over single-mandate districts, consistent with results in 2021 when it won 324 seats. Recent VCIOM and FOM polls (August 2024) show United Russia at 40-45% support, far ahead of rivals amid suppressed opposition following arrests of figures like Alexei Navalny and tightened electoral laws. New People (17.6%) gains traction from youth appeal and regional wins in 2023 gubernatorial races, boosting its party-list prospects under leader Alexei Nechayev. LDPR (7.0%) and KPRF (1.7%) trail due to post-Zhirinovsky leadership flux and static bases, while smaller parties like SRZP, Rodina, and Civic Platform face steep barriers. Upcoming party congresses and candidate approvals could shift dynamics before the 2026 vote.

question: Will Elon Musk rejoin the Trump Administration in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump officially nominates or appoints Elon Musk to his administration between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The Trump administration includes individuals formally appointed or nominated by Donald Trump to roles within the U.S. federal government, such as Cabinet members, Executive Office staff, senior policy advisors, ambassadors, or White House staff whose appointments are publicly announced by official government channels.

Nominations will qualify even if the individual is not confirmed.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will Elon Musk rejoin the Trump Administration in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump officially nominates or appoints Elon Musk to his administration between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The Trump administration includes individuals formally appointed or nominated by Donald Trump to roles within the U.S. federal government, such as Cabinet members, Executive Office staff, senior policy advisors, ambassadors, or White House staff whose appointments are publicly announced by official government channels.

Nominations will qualify even if the individual is not confirmed.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US government; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors "No" at 85.5% odds for Elon Musk rejoining the Trump Administration in 2026, driven primarily by Musk's repeated public statements prioritizing his CEO roles at Tesla, SpaceX, and xAI over extended government service. Recent developments reinforce this: Musk announced in May 2025 he's winding down his advisory role co-chairing the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), an external task force without official capacity, amid escalating demands from Tesla's Robotaxi launch delays and xAI's Grok model scaling. Conflicts of interest from federal contracts at SpaceX, plus ethics rules barring dual roles, further dim prospects, with no Trump signals of a formal position despite past rapport—traders eye 2026 midterms and Musk's Q4 earnings as key tests.

question: Will Morgan Wallen be the Billboard #1 top artist in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number 1 artist on the Billboard 2026 Year-End Top Artists chart.

If Billboard does not publish a 2026 Year-End Top Artists chart by March 31, 2027, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Billboard Year-End 2026 Top Artists Chart, once it becomes available.

events.title: Billboard #1 Artist 2026
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number 1 artist on the Billboard 2026 Year-End Top Artists chart.

If Billboard does not publish a 2026 Year-End Top Artists chart by March 31, 2027, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Billboard Year-End 2026 Top Artists Chart, once it becomes available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tyler, The Creator commands the highest implied probability at 29.9% for Billboard's 2026 #1 Artist thanks to Chromakopia's massive debut—298,000 equivalent album units in its first week—fueling trader optimism for his tour rollout and streaming endurance into next year. This wide-open field, with no one above 30%, reflects uncertainty over unannounced projects amid a fragmented landscape: pop juggernauts like Taylor Swift (10.9%) and Sabrina Carpenter (10.3%) rely on proven fanbases and radio play, while country heavyweights Morgan Wallen (9.5%) and Luke Combs (8.5%) leverage sales dominance; Bad Bunny (12.5%) and Olivia Rodrigo (12.3%) eye Latin-urban crossover and Gen-Z appeal. Key differentiators include 2026 release schedules, Coachella-scale tours, and Hot 100 longevity, as Billboard's multi-metric formula rewards consistent consumption amid volatile chart flux.

question: Will A$AP Rocky have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent chart surges have locked in trader consensus for artists like Taylor Swift, Sabrina Carpenter, Post Malone, Kendrick Lamar, and newcomers Lady Gaga and Bruno Mars with "Die With A Smile" debuting at #1 on the Hot 100 dated November 2, boosting their implied probabilities near 100%. Billie Eilish's "Birds of a Feather" lingers at #2 after 20 weeks in the top 10, fueling 60-70% odds amid streaming dominance from her Hit Me Hard and Soft era. With only eight weeks left in 2024, viral TikTok hits from Chappell Roan or Charli XCX face long odds without explosive radio airplay, while holiday releases pose upset risks per historical patterns like Mariah Carey's perennial spikes—traders eye next Friday's chart update closely.

question: Will Olivia Rodrigo have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

question: Will The Weeknd have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

question: Will the Doge-1 Lunar Mission launch before 2027?
description:
If the Doge-1 12U Lunar Cube satellite successfully launches from its launch pad by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Yes”. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The outcome of any launch will be corroborated by examining official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

Any subsequent anomaly (e.g., an explosion) after the launch will have no bearing on the outcome. Any name change of the satellite with another vehicle will have no bearing on the outcome of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Persistent delays in the Doge-1 CubeSat mission, a neutron spectrometer aimed at mapping lunar water ice, have solidified trader consensus at 96.3% for "No" launch before 2027. Originally targeting 2022 via SpaceX rideshare, the payload shifted to Intuitive Machines' IM-2 lander after multiple postponements, but IM-1's February 2024 partial failure—tipping over post-landing—highlighted landing risks and extended timelines. With no recent confirmations of Doge-1's integration or firm Falcon 9 slot amid SpaceX's packed manifest, NET late 2025 feels optimistic. Realistic Yes scenarios include accelerated IM-2 integration and launch by mid-2026, though project silence from Geometric Energy Corporation heightens skepticism.

question: Lai Ching-te out as President of Taiwan by December 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Lai Ching-te ceases to be President of Taiwan for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Lai Ching-te's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Lai Ching-te and the government of Taiwan; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Taiwan's constitutional impeachment process demands a three-fourths supermajority (76 of 113 seats) in the Legislative Yuan to propose removing President Lai Ching-te, a threshold the opposition KMT-TPP bloc (60 seats) cannot reach without unlikely defections from his DPP party. Recent legislative clashes over reforms in May 2024, including physical scuffles and bill passage against DPP opposition, have heightened political friction but produced no viable ouster mechanism or resignation pressure. Lai's approval ratings hover around 40-50% amid China military drills and earthquake recovery, yet cross-strait tensions have not destabilized his position. Traders' 91.5% "No" consensus reflects this entrenched structural barrier through 2026, barring unforeseen crises.

question: Human moon landing in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any human-crewed mission lands on the moon between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A touchdown of the spacecraft with humans aboard will be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of technical complications.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 96% implied probability for a human Moon landing in 2026, driven primarily by NASA's repeated delays in the Artemis program and SpaceX's Starship human landing system (HLS) challenges. Artemis II, the crewed orbital test, slipped to April 2025 due to Orion spacecraft issues like faulty valves and batteries, while GAO projections push Artemis III—the first landing mission relying on unproven Starship refueling and descent tech—to mid-2027 at earliest. Starship's explosive tests underscore propellant transfer and heat shield risks. Realistic upsets include accelerated orbital refueling demos or FAA fast-tracks, but historical NASA slips and regulatory hurdles like environmental reviews make a 2026 success improbable.

question: Will fewer than 10 albums be ranked #1 on the Billboard 200 in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of albums that are ranked number 1 on any official weekly Billboard 200 albums chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026.

Each specific album may only count once, regardless of the number times it reaches the number 1 spot. Different editions of the same album will be considered to be different specific albums if they contain different tracklists.

Billboard updates the Billboard 200 albums chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data from the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard 200 album chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard 200 album chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

All officially published Billboard 200 Albums charts will be treated as final. Revisions made after the release of a Billboard 200 album chart will not be considered.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official weekly Billboard 200 albums chart, published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/billboard-200/ and through other official Billboard channels; however, a consensus of credible reporting on albums that reached number 1 on the Billboard 200 within this market's timeframe may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment clusters around 140-179 SpaceX launches in 2026 (69% combined implied probability), propelled by Falcon 9's record 2024 cadence—projected at 144 orbital attempts—and Starship's Flight 5 successes in heat shield integrity and booster recovery, signaling reusable rocket scalability. The tight 140-159 (35.8%) versus 160-179 (33.0%) split hinges on Starship's propulsion reliability and rapid turnaround times, with FAA licensing for frequent flights as a key differentiator; 200+ (19.5%) assumes 5-10 daily operations per engineering targets, but regulatory hurdles and supply chain limits cap lower bins amid unproven ship catches and Starlink deployment saturation.

This market will resolve according to the number of SpaceX launches between January 1, 2026, 12:00AM ET and December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET.

If the reported total number of SpaceX launches falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be https://www.spacex.com/launches.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment clusters around 140-179 SpaceX launches in 2026 (69% combined implied probability), propelled by Falcon 9's record 2024 cadence—projected at 144 orbital attempts—and Starship's Flight 5 successes in heat shield integrity and booster recovery, signaling reusable rocket scalability. The tight 140-159 (35.8%) versus 160-179 (33.0%) split hinges on Starship's propulsion reliability and rapid turnaround times, with FAA licensing for frequent flights as a key differentiator; 200+ (19.5%) assumes 5-10 daily operations per engineering targets, but regulatory hurdles and supply chain limits cap lower bins amid unproven ship catches and Starlink deployment saturation.

question: Will BRICS add a new member in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a new country accepts an invitation to join BRICS between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only new member states, not new partner states, will be considered toward this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this country will be information from BRICS members (e.g., https://brics.br/en/about-the-brics, etc.); however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will BRICS add a new member in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a new country accepts an invitation to join BRICS between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only new member states, not new partner states, will be considered toward this market's resolution.

The primary resolution source for this country will be information from BRICS members (e.g., https://brics.br/en/about-the-brics, etc.); however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors no new full BRICS member joining in 2026, with "No" at 57.5%, reflecting the Kazan summit's cautious approach to expansion announced in October 2024. Rather than adding full members, leaders formalized a "partner countries" tier—including Algeria, Belarus, Bolivia, Cuba, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Malaysia, Nigeria, Thailand, Turkey, Uganda, Uzbekistan, and Vietnam—to test alignment without immediate accession. Full membership demands unanimous consent from the current ten nations, a high bar amid divergent interests and external pressures like potential US sanctions on de-dollarization. The 2025 Rio de Janeiro summit may discuss progress, but traders see slowed momentum after January 2024's additions of Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, and UAE.

question: Will a country leave BRICS in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any member state formally withdraws from BRICS or provides an official notice of denunciation to BRICS between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal.

The resolution source will be official information from the relevant government and BRICS; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will a country leave BRICS in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any member state formally withdraws from BRICS or provides an official notice of denunciation to BRICS between market creation and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal.

The resolution source will be official information from the relevant government and BRICS; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors no BRICS departure in 2026 at 68.5% implied probability, driven by the absence of official statements or actions from member states—Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa, Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, and the UAE—indicating exit intentions. The group's recent Kazan summit in October 2024 reinforced unity through expansion talks, inviting 13 partner countries like Algeria and Vietnam, underscoring deepening economic ties and de-dollarization goals despite geopolitical frictions, such as India-China border tensions. No credible rumors or policy shifts suggest withdrawals, with historical cohesion prevailing amid divergent member interests, positioning stability as the base rate for market pricing.

question: Will Declan Rice win the 2026 PFA Player of the Year?
description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2025-26 PFA Players' Player of the Year.

If two or more players are announced as winners of the 2026 PFA Players’ Player of the Year award, this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no 2025-2026 PFA Players' Player of the Year winner is declared by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the Professional Footballers’ Association; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: 2025-2026 PFA Players' Player of the Year Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2025-26 PFA Players' Player of the Year.

If two or more players are announced as winners of the 2026 PFA Players’ Player of the Year award, this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If no 2025-2026 PFA Players' Player of the Year winner is declared by August 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source will be official information from the Professional Footballers’ Association; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Declan Rice leads trader consensus at 59.5% for the 2025-26 PFA Players' Player of the Year, fueled by his commanding midfield presence at Arsenal, where his defensive recoveries, progressive passes, and goal contributions have anchored their Premier League title push amid a formidable run of form. Bruno Fernandes trails at 25%, buoyed by his creative output and leadership at Manchester United despite inconsistent team results. Erling Haaland's 8.6% reflects his perennial goal-scoring prowess at City, while Aston Villa's Morgan Rogers matches it with breakout attacking flair. Rising prospects like Chelsea-bound Estevao (7.7%) and rumored Arsenal target Martin Zubimendi (7.4%) gain traction on transfer buzz and youth potential, though long-term voter sentiment hinges on sustained impact, team success, and individual stats next season.

question: Will China's annual inflation in 2026 be less than -1.0%?
description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in China over the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) of China.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) during the 12-month period ending December 2026 according to the monthly NBS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the NBS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026 (https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/), currently expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find the relevant figure by locating the report for December 2026 on the Press Releases page (see: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/), locating the table named "Consumer Price Indexes in December 2026", and finding the consumer price index figure in the column labeled "Growth Rate Y/Y (%)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly NBS CPI news release which reports inflation during 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/ReleaseCalendar/

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for China's 2026 annual inflation clusters tightly around subdued outcomes, with 0.1-0.5% at 16% implied probability edging out 1.1-1.5% (15%) and 2.5%+ (14.4%), signaling trader consensus on persistent lowflation risks. Driving this are August 2024 CPI readings of -0.5% year-over-year—marking five straight months of deflation—fueled by property sector distress, tepid consumer spending, and excess capacity, despite PBOC rate cuts and fiscal stimulus pledges. Forward projections from major institutions anticipate a gradual pickup to 1-2% amid potential demand recovery, but competitive dynamics hinge on stimulus transmission efficacy versus global commodity pressures; key watchpoints include Q4 GDP releases and January 2025 Politburo signals for reflation catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for China's 2026 annual inflation clusters tightly around subdued outcomes, with 0.1-0.5% at 16% implied probability edging out 1.1-1.5% (15%) and 2.5%+ (14.4%), signaling trader consensus on persistent lowflation risks. Driving this are August 2024 CPI readings of -0.5% year-over-year—marking five straight months of deflation—fueled by property sector distress, tepid consumer spending, and excess capacity, despite PBOC rate cuts and fiscal stimulus pledges. Forward projections from major institutions anticipate a gradual pickup to 1-2% amid potential demand recovery, but competitive dynamics hinge on stimulus transmission efficacy versus global commodity pressures; key watchpoints include Q4 GDP releases and January 2025 Politburo signals for reflation catalysts.

question: Will China's annual inflation in 2026 be between 1.6% and 2.0%?
description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in China over the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) of China.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) during the 12-month period ending December 2026 according to the monthly NBS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the NBS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026 (https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/), currently expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find the relevant figure by locating the report for December 2026 on the Press Releases page (see: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/), locating the table named "Consumer Price Indexes in December 2026", and finding the consumer price index figure in the column labeled "Growth Rate Y/Y (%)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly NBS CPI news release which reports inflation during 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.stats.gov.cn/english/PressRelease/ReleaseCalendar/

question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation be less than 20%?
description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' tight clustering of odds around 20-35% for Argentina's 2026 annual inflation—led by 20-24.9% at 28.5%, 25-29.9% at 25.5%, and 30-34.9% at 22.1%—signals strong consensus on President Milei's disinflation reforms sustaining momentum beyond 2025. Recent monthly CPI eased to 2.4% in October from 3.5% prior, dragging yearly inflation to 193% from peaks above 270%, while IMF projections peg end-2025 at ~29% and central bank targets imply sub-25% for 2026 amid fiscal surpluses and a crawling peso peg. Competitive dynamics hinge on fiscal discipline versus shocks like commodity slumps or drought; resolution thresholds favor aggressive bets on 25-30% if Q1 2025 prints hold below 2.5% monthly, underscoring capital-weighted trader bets on policy endurance over historical volatility.

question: Fresno State Bulldogs vs. Utah State Aggies
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for January 14 at 12:00 AM ET:

If the Fresno State Bulldogs win, the market will resolve to "Fresno State Bulldogs".

If the Utah State Aggies win, the market will resolve to "Utah State Aggies".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.title: Fresno State Bulldogs vs. Utah State Aggies
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between Fresno State Bulldogs and Utah State Aggies on January 14 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for the Utah State Aggies' narrow 51% implied edge over the Fresno State Bulldogs stems primarily from the Aggies' strong home-field advantage at Maverik Stadium in Logan, where they've won four straight conference games dating back to last season. Both Mountain West contenders sit at 3-2 overall, with Fresno State riding momentum from a gritty road win at Nevada but vulnerable on the road against physical run defenses like Utah State's. Recent developments include no major injuries reported on official updates, though Aggies QB Logan Fife's mobility could exploit Fresno's secondary if healthy. Odds could shift on Friday's injury report or weather forecasts, as high elevation and wind often amplify upsets in this rivalry matchup.

question: ICE shooter charged by March 31?
description:
On January 7, 2026, an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer committed a shooting in Minneapolis (see: https://apnews.com/article/minnesota-immigration-enforcement-shooting-crackdown-surge-173e00fa7388054e98c3b5b9417c1e5a).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of the ICE Officer who fired the shots in the specified shooting, for any alleged crime relating to the shooting, between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For a “Yes” resolution, the charged individual does not need to be an active ICE officer at the time the charges are made, as long as they were the shooter in the specified event.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: ICE shooter charged by March 31?
events.description:
On January 7, 2026, an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer committed a shooting in Minneapolis (see: https://apnews.com/article/minnesota-immigration-enforcement-shooting-crackdown-surge-173e00fa7388054e98c3b5b9417c1e5a).

This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of the ICE Officer who fired the shots in the specified shooting, for any alleged crime relating to the shooting, between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For a “Yes” resolution, the charged individual does not need to be an active ICE officer at the time the charges are made, as long as they were the shooter in the specified event.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors "No" at 98.1% for the ICE shooter being charged by March 31, driven by over five weeks since the February 18, 2024, incident at the Irving, Texas, ICE facility where Franklin Brown was arrested minutes after firing multiple rounds, yet no formal federal indictment has followed the initial complaint. Prosecutors appear to be weighing evidence amid ongoing investigation into motive and stolen firearm possession, with no public updates signaling imminent action. High confidence stems from typical federal timelines exceeding 40 days for grand jury review and historical base rates where preliminary holds rarely lead to rushed charges absent new catalysts. Realistic shifts could arise from fresh forensic results or witness testimony prompting DOJ filing before deadline, though political sensitivities around immigration enforcement add unpredictability.

question: ICE Shooter fired/resigns by March 31?
description:
On January 7, 2026, an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer committed a shooting in Minneapolis (see: https://apnews.com/article/minnesota-immigration-enforcement-shooting-crackdown-surge-173e00fa7388054e98c3b5b9417c1e5a).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the ICE Officer who fired the shots in the specified shooting ceases to be an employee of ICE for any length of time between market creation and March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement of the officer’s resignation/firing before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/firing goes into effect.

Suspensions, administrative leave, or other temporary measures will not qualify.

This market's primary resolution source will be official information from ICE and the U.S. federal government; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Historical precedents for similar use-of-force cases show rare pre-investigation terminations. Realistic shifts could arise from unexpected investigative findings, video evidence release, or sudden civil rights lawsuits prompting resignation, though time constraints limit feasibility.

question: Anyone charged for doxing Delta Force commander heading Maduro grab by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any federal or state jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of any individual in connection with the alleged doxing of the Delta Force commander associated with Operation Absolute Resolve by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, “charged” includes the filing of criminal charges, indictments, or the unsealing of a criminal complaint. Civil lawsuits alone do not qualify.

For the purposes of this market, the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a U.S. state shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' overwhelming consensus for no charges by March 31 reflects the lack of any announced federal investigation or indictment against Ken Klippenstein, who publicly identified the Delta Force lieutenant colonel linked to a reported U.S. operation targeting Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro. Since the March 2024 disclosure—based on Seymour Hersh's reporting—the Pentagon has condemned the doxing as reckless but taken no visible legal steps, with the Justice Department silent amid free speech debates for journalists. With months elapsed and no momentum toward prosecution, odds imply minimal risk of action before the deadline. A realistic shift could arise from an abrupt DoJ filing or heightened national security scrutiny, though current inaction sustains high confidence in the "No" outcome.

question: Will Mariah Carey have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent chart surges have locked in trader consensus for artists like Taylor Swift, Sabrina Carpenter, Post Malone, Kendrick Lamar, and newcomers Lady Gaga and Bruno Mars with "Die With A Smile" debuting at #1 on the Hot 100 dated November 2, boosting their implied probabilities near 100%. Billie Eilish's "Birds of a Feather" lingers at #2 after 20 weeks in the top 10, fueling 60-70% odds amid streaming dominance from her Hit Me Hard and Soft era. With only eight weeks left in 2024, viral TikTok hits from Chappell Roan or Charli XCX face long odds without explosive radio airplay, while holiday releases pose upset risks per historical patterns like Mariah Carey's perennial spikes—traders eye next Friday's chart update closely.

question: Masoud Pezeshkian out by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Masoud Pezeshkian ceases to be President of Iran for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Masoud Pezeshkian's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Masoud Pezeshkian and the government of Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Masoud Pezeshkian's potential removal from Iran's presidency leans toward stability, with low implied probabilities for near-term ouster reflecting his recent cabinet confirmations by the conservative-dominated parliament. Pezeshkian, a reformist elected in July 2024, secured approval for his remaining ministers in late October after initial rejections of nominees like the foreign minister, signaling tentative institutional acceptance amid tensions over policy directions. Key risks stem from parliamentary oversight, Guardian Council vetoes, and Supreme Leader Khamenei's influence, exacerbated by regional escalations including Iran's October missile strikes on Israel and vows of further response. Upcoming budget debates and nuclear negotiations could test his coalition, but no confirmed impeachment proceedings exist as of now.

question: Over $140B wagered on US sports betting in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the total amount wagered on U.S. sports betting in 2026 is greater than the number specified in the title. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source will be Legal Sports Report (https://www.legalsportsreport.com/sports-betting-states/revenue/), using the “U.S. sports betting revenue by month” table. The annual data will be calculated by summing the values in the “Handle” column for all months in 2026 once the relevant information is available.

If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, the market will resolve based on other reliable sources

eventMetadata.context_description: Surging legalization across more states drives trader consensus toward record US sports betting handle in 2026, with 2024 totals already topping $150 billion per American Gaming Association data, up 20% from 2023. Recent launches in North Carolina (March 2024, $500M+ first month) and Nebraska highlight momentum, while online platforms like FanDuel and DraftKings capture growing mobile wagering. Projections from Eilers & Krejcik Gaming forecast $180-200B by 2026 amid NFL/NBA popularity, but regulatory stalls in California/Texas and economic slowdowns pose downside risks, tempering aggressive bets on the highest brackets.

question: Memphis Tigers vs. Utah State Aggies
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for January 15 at 12:00 AM ET:

If the Memphis Tigers win, the market will resolve to "Memphis Tigers".

If the Utah State Aggies win, the market will resolve to "Utah State Aggies".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.title: Memphis Tigers vs. Utah State Aggies
events.description:
This event is for the CBB game between Memphis Tigers and Utah State Aggies on January 15 at 12:00 AM ET.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Utah State's slight 51% implied probability edge stems from their superior 27-8 record and Mountain West Tournament title, showcasing elite road form (14-4 away) against a Memphis team hosting at FedEx Forum after a 99-88 NIT first-round rout of Texas A&M. Competitive balance arises from Memphis's home-court advantage, deeper bench, and Jaykwon Walton's scoring punch (19.9 PPG), offsetting Utah State's Great Osobor-led frontcourt dominance (18.2 PPG, 9.5 RPG). Recent momentum favors the Aggies' 99-82 UCI win, but a Walton injury flare-up or Memphis crowd surge could flip odds toward the Tigers, while Aggies' perimeter shooting (37% from three) tips it their way if clicking.

question: X banned in U.K. by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if use of X/Twitter is banned within the United Kingdom by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A ban will qualify if legislation is enacted or government action is taken to bar U.K. citizens from downloading and/or viewing X/Twitter, and/or posting on X/Twitter. Any legislation or government action that meets these standards will qualify, regardless of if or when the ban goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the United Kingdom and X/Twitter, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus at 99.2% "No" on an X ban in the U.K. by March 31 stems primarily from the absence of any official regulatory filings, court orders, or legislative proposals targeting the platform's outright prohibition, despite past tensions over content moderation during the 2024 Southport riots. U.K. authorities, enforcing the Online Safety Act, have prioritized fines and age verification mandates over bans, with no escalated actions announced as the deadline nears. X's partial compliance and Elon Musk's vocal pushback further deter extreme measures. Realistic risks remain slim—such as unforeseen emergency legislation amid new unrest or a High Court injunction—but historical precedent shows U.K. regulators favoring targeted enforcement, sustaining this near-certain market-implied probability.

question: Will Satoshi move any Bitcoin in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any wallet labeled as belonging to Satoshi Nakamoto on Arkham’s Intel Explorer shows an “Outflow” or “Swaps” transaction at any time between January 9, 2026, 1:00 PM ET and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Arkham’s Intel Explorer, specifically the entity page for Satoshi Nakamoto available at https://intel.arkm.com/explorer/entity/satoshi-nakamoto

If Arkham becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on a consensus of credible sources.

question: Odds Trump acquires Greenland before 2027 hit 50% by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the “Yes” option for the “Will Trump acquire Greenland before 2027?” market  (https://polymarket.com/event/will-trump-acquire-greenland-before-2027) is priced over the listed value for any four-hour period ending by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or earlier. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A tie in the number of minutes will not suffice.

The resolution source will be the underlying minute-level price data for the “Will Trump acquire Greenland before 2027?” market, which can be viewed using the bar chart on https://derivative.polymarket.com/will-trump-acquire-greenland-before-2027-over-50 or through the “Resolution” tab with the time set to show the four-hour window. Both interfaces display the same underlying data.

Note: This market will resolve according to the price scoring methodology of derivative.polymarket.com.

question: Main Street Sports Sale/Merger by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if it is announced that Main Street Sports will be, has been, or is being acquired by or merged with any other company or private individual(s) by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An announcement by Main Street Sports or the other company involved will qualify for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether the announced acquisition/merger actually occurs.

Partial sales may count, as long as the acquiring company acquires a controlling interest in Main Street Sports. A “controlling interest” refers to a change in ownership sufficient to control the company’s strategic decisions (typically more than 50% of equity, or equivalent control via voting and governance rights). Transactions or investments that do not result in a transfer of controlling interest will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Main Street Sports and the acquiring company; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
